description_long: This radio receiver features a rectangular body with a brushed metal top and front panel, complemented by wooden side panels that add a vintage aesthetic. The front face is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, toggle switches, and a large tuning dial, as well as a horizontal blue-lit frequency display and a small analog meter. The top surface has several ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The rear panel is outfitted with a variety of RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, clearly labeled for left and right channels. The overall design is reminiscent of classic hi-fi stereo receivers, with a robust and functional appearance. The materials and build suggest durability and a focus on audio performance.
description: A vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wooden sides, multiple control knobs, and a blue-lit frequency display.
description_view_0: The front view shows the brushed metal faceplate, blue-lit frequency display, analog meter, large tuning knob, and several smaller control knobs and switches.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wooden panel and the protruding control knobs, with ventilation slits visible on the top.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the input/output ports, speaker terminals, and additional labeling, with the metal and wood construction visible.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wooden panel and the ends of the control knobs, with top ventilation slits.
